capabilities: Combine event sourcing with key-based data partitioning to optimize SQL read models, improving query efficiency and scalability.

procedure.action: Run EXPLAIN ANALYZE on the 10 slowest queries and extract columns appearing in WHERE clauses across >60% of queries.

Partition tables using the extracted columns as partition keys to distribute data evenly across partitions using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Instantiate indexes on partition keys to speed up query execution and reduce scan times by instantiating the resource with the specified configuration; assert resource_id != null and resource_state = 'active'; log creation_duration_ms to audit_log.

Invoke pg_partman or similar tools to automate partition management and maintenance by invoking the target API with the specified parameters; assert response_status < 400 and response_time_ms < 5000; log request_id and response_code.

Set up monitoring with pg_stat_statements to track query performance and adjust partitioning strategy as needed using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

summary: Enhance SQL read models in event sourcing by applying key-based data partitioning for efficient query pruning, ensuring scalable and performant data retrieval.
title: Optimizing SQL Read Models with Key-Based Data Partitioning

failureConditions: Increased query latency due to inefficient partitioning.
Uneven data distribution leading to hotspots.
Complexity in partition management causing maintenance overhead.
successConditions: Verify p95 query time is reduced by at least 30% after partitioning
Ensure no single partition exceeds 20% of total data volume
Check that query execution plans use partition pruning effectively
